title: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Implement a Node.js Express middleware route that handles GET requests to '/users/:name'. The route should validate if the provided ':name' parameter is a string. If it is, the route should return a JSON object with a 'message' property containing the provided name. If the parameter is not a string, the route should return a 400 Bad Request error with a JSON body containing an error message.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the request path.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be either a JSON object with a 'message' property or a JSON object with an 'error' property and a 400 Bad Request status code.
statement.notes: The route should handle requests to '/users/:name' where ':name' can be any string.

constraints.description: The input string length will be between 1 and 10000 characters.

examples.explanation: The route should return a JSON object with a 'message' property containing the provided name.
The route should return a JSON object with an 'error' property and a 400 Bad Request status code.

hints.text: Use the express.Router().param() method to validate the ':name' parameter.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The express.Router().param() method is used to validate route parameters. It takes a regular expression as an argument to match the parameter value.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve checking the type of the parameter value in the route handler function.
editorial.betterSolution: Use express.Router().param() to validate the parameter value.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use express.Router().param() with a regular expression to validate the parameter value.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is correct as it uses the express.Router().param() method to validate the parameter value.
commonMistakes: Not using express.Router().param() to validate the parameter value.
Not using a regular expression to validate the parameter value.
